05.1

TECHNICAL VIGNETTES (Part A)

 

Kärsämäki SHINGLE CHURCH, FINLAND

Project Brief: The first part of this studio focused on technical vignettes that analyzed building conditions from ground to roof. I was assigned Kärsämäki Church in Finland, a project known for its materiality, structure, and unique spatial qualities.

 

Using images, plans, and sections, I studied how the church was organized and how its ground, wall, span, and roof conditions worked together. Each vignette was refined weekly through feedback from instructors and classmates. This process strengthened my technical drawing skills and helped me better understand how material, structure, and space connect within a building.

 

INDIVIDUAL PROJECT

5.1: Ground Vignette / Scale 1/2”=1’

5.3: Span Vignette / Scale 1/2”=1’

5.4: Roof Vignette / Scale 1”=1’

5.5: West Entrance Render (Photoshop)

5.6: Building Vignette / Scale 1/2”=1’

 

*all vignettes completed in Rhino 7

05.2

SDSU AGRICULTURAL HERITAGE MUSEUM - Extension (Part B)

 

BROOKINGS, SD

Project Brief: Following the vignette studies, we formed teams to design an extension for the Agriculture Heritage Museum at SDSU. Our team developed floor plans, sections, vignettes, renderings, and a physical model to communicate a shared design vision.

 

The project required collaboration across Rhino, Revit, Photoshop, Illustrator, and physical model making. It was an important step in learning how to move from individual analysis into a larger team based design process.

 

Group Members:

Katie Ishol

Manahil Elsheikh

Cheyenne Miller
